# Sentinel Snapshot
<!-- Auto-generated by sentinel scan on 2026-06-18. Edit freely - sentinel evolve will reconcile. -->

## Tech Stack
A macOS-only, Apple Silicon dotfiles environment deployed via GNU Stow across 22 stowable packages. The primary languages are Zsh/Bash (shell config and automation scripts), Lua (Neovim plugins via lazy.nvim), and TOML (AeroSpace, Starship). TypeScript utility scripts run under Bun with no npm dependencies; a small Astro/React docs site lives under site/. Key tools include Neovim, Tmux, Ghostty, AeroSpace (tiling WM), Sketchybar (status bar), mise (polyglot version manager), and BetterDisplay for external display management.

## Architecture
The repository is a collection of 22 modular GNU Stow packages, each a self-contained configuration unit whose internal directory tree mirrors its target location under $HOME. Deployment is fully idempotent: install.sh drives Homebrew, stow, mise, TPM, and lazy.nvim in sequence with existence guards at every step. Shell automation scripts under scripts/scripts/ follow a consistent style (kebab-case names, set -u/-e, personal.env overrides, mkdir-based locking) and are executed either directly from .zshrc aliases, as launchd agents, or via Sketchybar plugins. Sketchybar is the integration hub connecting AeroSpace workspace events, BetterDisplay display modes, OBS, Spotify, system stats, and Claude Code billing — items declared in items/ and driven by event scripts in plugins/. Neovim configuration is namespaced under lua/sethy/ and uses lazy.nvim with one-file-per-plugin specs, while the broader environment is themed Catppuccin Mocha for terminals and Rose-pine for Neovim.

## Conventions
- **file_organization** (conf 1): Each top-level directory is a GNU Stow package whose internal tree mirrors the target filesystem verbatim (e.g. aerospace/.config/aerospace/aerospace.toml → ~/.config/aerospace/aerospace.toml). No flattening or aliasing allowed.
- **naming** (conf 0.95): All shell and TypeScript scripts use kebab-case filenames (e.g. bd-apply.sh, streamdeck-build.ts, obs-scene-build.ts). No snake_case or camelCase script names.
- **naming** (conf 0.98): Shell functions use snake_case exclusively.
- **naming** (conf 0.92): Overridable per-rig environment variables are prefixed DOTFILES_ in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Internal script vars are UPPER_SNAKE_CASE without prefix.
- **other** (conf 0.9): Scripts that accept per-rig configuration source ~/.config/dotfiles/personal.env at the top with a file-existence guard, before any variable use.
- **error_handling** (conf 0.88): All bash scripts declare set -e (exit on error) or set -u (treat unset vars as errors) immediately after the shebang and any leading comments. Never both together unless explicitly intended.
- **other** (conf 0.93): Shell scripts use #!/usr/bin/env bash shebang. Exception: scripts invoked directly by launchd (which uses macOS stock /bin/bash 3.2) must use #!/bin/bash and avoid bash 4+ features like declare -A.
- **other** (conf 0.9): Pipe-delimited string tables (not associative arrays) are used for multi-column lookup data in scripts that may run under bash 3.2 (launchd). mode_row() parses via grep+substring.
- **error_handling** (conf 0.95): Concurrent-access serialization uses an atomic mkdir mutex (not flock, which is absent on macOS). Lock dirs store a PID file for stale-lock reclaim. Timeout after 30s proceeds rather than drops.
- **other** (conf 1): External tool initializations in .zshrc are guarded with `command -v <tool> &>/dev/null` before eval, preventing errors on machines where the tool is absent.
- **file_organization** (conf 0.97): Neovim plugin configs each live in their own file under lua/sethy/plugins/ and return a single lazy.nvim plugin spec table (return { ... }).
- **file_organization** (conf 1): Sketchybar configuration splits into items/ (item declarations, sourced from sketchybarrc) and plugins/ (event-driven scripts referenced by --script). Colors and icons are centralized in colors.sh and icons.sh.
- **other** (conf 0.92): TypeScript utility scripts use #!/usr/bin/env bun shebang and prefer Node built-in modules over npm packages. Design tokens are defined as `const TOKENS = { ... } as const` at the top of scripts that generate UI assets.
- **other** (conf 0.97): Install/setup scripts are fully idempotent: every install action is guarded by a prior existence check (brew_installed, cask_installed, cmd_exists, [ -d ... ]). No action is applied twice.
- **file_organization** (conf 0.9): Per-tool documentation lives in docs/<tool>/README.md. Cross-cutting memory/decisions go in MEMORY/*.md with an index in MEMORY/MEMORY.md.
- **other** (conf 0.9): AeroSpace workspace-to-monitor assignments use quoted regex patterns ('^PORTRAIT-MONITOR$') for external monitors and plain strings ('Built-in Retina Display') for the built-in, enabling portable configs across monitor renames.
- **other** (conf 0.95): BetterDisplay CLI writes are guarded by readback verification with up to 3 retry attempts before logging a WARN. The exit-0 lie of `betterdisplaycli set` makes readback mandatory.
- **styling** (conf 0.95): Catppuccin Mocha is the theme for terminal-facing tools (Ghostty, Tmux, Zsh/Starship); Rose-pine is used for Neovim. Sketchybar uses a custom Durante dark palette with PURE_BLACK backgrounds and TRANSPARENT bar.

## Key Decisions
- **GNU Stow is the sole deployment mechanism. Every package's directory tree exactly mirrors the install target so stow can create symlinks without any path translation logic.** — Stow provides idempotent, reversible symlinking with package-level granularity (stow/unstow individual tools). The mirrored-path convention is the non-negotiable prerequisite for this to work.
- **Bun is the runtime for all TypeScript utility scripts, not Node. No npm packages are used in scripts; only built-in Node/Bun APIs.** — Bun provides faster startup than Node for one-shot scripts, and eliminating npm deps removes the need for node_modules in the repo. Stated explicitly in streamdeck-build.ts.
- **Oh-My-Zsh was removed from the shell configuration in favor of manually defined aliases and tool-native integrations (starship, zoxide, direnv, mise, fzf, atuin).** — OMZ was adding ~200ms to shell startup. All needed functionality (git aliases, web-search) was replicated manually. Comment in .zshrc explicitly records the decision and savings.
- **mise replaces all single-language version managers (fnm, pyenv, nvm). A single `mise activate zsh` call handles all runtimes via .mise.toml, .tool-versions, .nvmrc, and .python-version.** — Reduces shell init overhead (one eval vs three), unifies per-project tooling declaration in a single file, and removes fnm/pyenv/nvm from the Brewfile.
- **BetterDisplay mode-switching writes display parameters directly via CLI rather than loading named 'favorite mode' slots, because the slot-load mechanism is broken in BetterDisplay 4.3.0 pre-release.** — Slot loads were unreliable in the pre-release channel. Direct parameter writes with readback verification are the bulletproof path. Documented as a temporary workaround pending channel revert.
- **bash 3.2 compatibility is maintained for any script that may be invoked by macOS launchd, using pipe-delimited string tables instead of associative arrays and avoiding bash 4+ syntax.** — launchd on macOS uses /bin/bash which is frozen at 3.2 due to GPL licensing. Scripts must run without error in that environment.
- **Per-rig personal configuration is isolated to ~/.config/dotfiles/personal.env (untracked), sourced with an existence guard. All overridable values carry DOTFILES_-prefixed defaults in the script itself.** — Keeps machine-specific values (display tag IDs, brightness preferences) out of version control while preserving a single canonical script shared across machines.

## Setup
- Install: `./install.sh`
- Dev: `zsh -i`
- Build: `bun run scripts/scripts/streamdeck-build.ts`
- Test: `# No test suite — use VERIFY.md: bash VERIFY.md manually`
- Lint: `# CI: see .github/workflows/lint.yml`
